raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.5", "R1.6", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What specific aspects do you want checked primarily: writing polish and grammar (focus on R1.6), logical consistency and evidence alignment (focus on R1.2 and R1.3), novelty and citations (focus on R1.4 and R1.7), or structural organization (focus on R1.5)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Checking the paper draft centrally involves verifying logical consistency of the argument chain.",
    "R1.3": "The delegation implies aligning claims with reported evidence as a key aspect of the check.",
    "R1.4": "Assessing novelty against prior work is a peripheral but relevant part of reviewing the draft's claims.",
    "R1.5": "Evaluating structural organization at the section level is nice-to-have for an overall check of the artifact.",
    "R1.6": "Polishing writing at the sentence and paragraph level is a central expectation when asked to check a draft.",
    "R1.7": "Checking citation accuracy and scholarship coverage is peripheral given the artifact's lack of explicit references.",
    "RX.1": "Disclosing uncertainties is a cross-cutting responsibility expected in any check of research claims.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaims is centrally important when reviewing the draft's stated advances and results.",
    "RX.3": "Adhering to the stated scope is a key check to ensure the artifact doesn't stray from its focus.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding downstream harms is always considered in checking AI-related research for potential misuses.",
    "RX.5": "Ensuring provenance and traceability is relevant for verifying the origins of models and data mentioned."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What specific aspects do you want checked primarily: writing polish and grammar (focus on R1.6), logical consistency and evidence alignment (focus on R1.2 and R1.3), novelty and citations (focus on R1.4 and R1.7), or structural organization (focus on R1.5)?

rationale.R1.2: Checking the paper draft centrally involves verifying logical consistency of the argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation implies aligning claims with reported evidence as a key aspect of the check.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing novelty against prior work is a peripheral but relevant part of reviewing the draft's claims.
rationale.R1.5: Evaluating structural organization at the section level is nice-to-have for an overall check of the artifact.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing writing at the sentence and paragraph level is a central expectation when asked to check a draft.
rationale.R1.7: Checking citation accuracy and scholarship coverage is peripheral given the artifact's lack of explicit references.
rationale.RX.1: Disclosing uncertainties is a cross-cutting responsibility expected in any check of research claims.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is centrally important when reviewing the draft's stated advances and results.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the stated scope is a key check to ensure the artifact doesn't stray from its focus.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding downstream harms is always considered in checking AI-related research for potential misuses.
rationale.RX.5: Ensuring provenance and traceability is relevant for verifying the origins of models and data mentioned.
